item.title: Hedge fund Caxton extends losses to $1.3bn as Iran war rocks markets

item.description: Company potentially targeting $75-billion raise amid surging investor interest in the space sector

item.description: Some countries across the continent are imposing fines on people hoarding petrol, or subsidising fuel prices

item.description: Move aimed at assessing whether importing offshore wind could help meet Quebec’s growing electricity demand

item.description: Chinese maker of global hit doll is world’s most valuable toy company

item.description: Traders expect prices to fall later this year, giving carriers hope they can lock in lower rates by being patient

item.description: Investor disapproval of possible tie-up reflects the challenge of integrating two different family controlled companies

item.description: While problems with the financing source are emerging, the region needs to diversify from bank funding

item.description: Investment group Tengelmann working on a proposal that could lead to a spin-off as soon as this year

item.description: Rightwing populist party is shifting back to ‘small state’ economic policies

item.description: Approval for online financial services platform permits it only to offer contracts tied to economic indicators, financial markets and climate trends

item.description: The new commercial terminal, located at the Saint-Hubert airfield in Longueuil, aims to draw four million passengers annually

item.description: Demand growing for immunoglobulin to address immune deficiencies that can result from cancer treatment

item.description: Summer jobs are hard to come by but jobs around the house such as lawn mowing and raking are easy enough for kids to pick up
